Tests the behavior of `Math.round` with negative zero in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of JavaScript's `Math.round` function when applied to negative zero. The assertion checks that dividing 1 by the result of `Math.round(-0)` yields `-Infinity`. This test verifies the correct handling of edge cases involving negative zero in mathematical operations, ensuring that the JavaScript engine adheres to the expected IEEE 754 floating-point arithmetic standards. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical behavior.
